question: Consider the two-dimensional Ising model on an L x L square lattice with periodic boundary conditions. The Hamiltonian includes both ferromagnetic nearest-neighbor exchange and long-range antiferromagnetic dipolar interactions: H = -delta * sum_{<i,j>NN} sigma_i sigma_j + sum_{i<j} sigma_i sigma_j / r_{ij}^3, where sigma_i = +/-1, delta is the nearest-neighbor exchange strength, r_{ij} is the distance between sites i and j (in lattice units), and the dipolar sum runs over all distinct pairs. At delta = 1.00, in the h=1 stripe phase (h is the stripe modulation PERIOD in lattice spacings: h=1 = alternating up/down rows, ordering wavevector (pi,pi); h is NOT a magnetic field), what is the infinite-volume critical temperature T_c^0 (= 1/beta_c^0, with k_B=1, in units of the energy coefficients of the Hamiltonian as written above), obtained via finite-size extrapolation of the real part of the leading partition-function zero? Output a JSON object {"Tc0": <value>}.

metadata.system: 2D dipolar Ising L x L PBC, delta=1.00, stripe phase h=1
metadata.difficulty_note: 2D dipolar+exchange Ising on square lattice (delta=1.00), partition-function zero FSS with multicanonical MC
